body:
This PR:
 - Moves some code loading, to make sure that we're overloading some OrdinaryDiffEq methods (`SplitFunction`). It turns out that it was, but moving this code up is also good because it confirms that there's no closures in these functions.
 - Hacks `OrdinaryDiffEq.calc_tderivative!` to skip calling `f` (this presumably is not needed when no explicit time derivatives exist).

It looks like:
 - All of our regression tests are passing, which is great
 - One additional RHS eval is eliminated (so we get a ~30% speedup)
title: Make sure overloading, hack `calc_tderivative!`

body: This PR adds a flame graph for internal energy, in attempts to understand why the internal energy long run is significantly faster than the total energy one.
title: Add internal energy flame graph

body: In #739, I forgot that the parsed args has all entries, so merging resulted in exactly the default configuration, what I want there is actually just non-defaults, so that overrides can be done on an existing config.
title: Fix flame graphs
